rubric_results.check: Title is non-empty and plausibly the document's title?
rubric_results.note: Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant EIS is a known NRC document; title is plausible and well-formed.

rubric_results.note: Short, descriptive title of appropriate length for an EIS document.

rubric_results.note: Only one source (NUL) provided; no second source to compare.

title.notes: The extracted title 'Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant : environmental impact statement.' is plausible and well-formed, consistent with standard NRC EIS titling conventions. Only one source was cited (NUL) with no cited page text provided for verification. Confidence remains high given the recognizable document type and naming pattern, but absence of a second source or cited page text prevents full corroboration.

rubric_results.check: Year falls within 1969 ≤ year ≤ current year?

rubric_results.check: If NUL and regex disagree, value reflects this with a low-confidence flag?
rubric_results.note: Extraction correctly flags disagreement between NUL=2026 and regex=1985 with low confidence

year.notes: There is a direct conflict between NUL metadata (2026, a future year outside valid range) and regex extraction (1985, a plausible EIS year). No cited page text was provided to adjudicate the disagreement. The regex value of 1985 is more plausible for an EIS document, but cannot be confirmed without source text; human review is required.

rubric_results.check: Type is one of allowed values
rubric_results.note: Final is a valid EIS type in the rubric

rubric_results.check: Regex and Sonnet agree
rubric_results.note: Both regex (first page) and Sonnet (first 2 pages) are listed as sources and both support Final

rubric_results.check: Evidence phrase supports chosen type
rubric_results.note: Phrase 'Final environmental statement was sent to the Environmental Protection Agency and made available to the public on January 30, 1978' explicitly uses the word Final and describes a completed/published EIS

eis_type.notes: The extracted value of 'Final' is well-supported by the evidence phrase, which explicitly references a 'Final environmental statement' submitted to the EPA in 1978. Both extraction methods agree. The only minor caveat is that no cited page text was provided for independent verification, so the evidence relies entirely on the extracted snippet rather than direct document inspection.

rubric_results.note: The agency name 'Tennessee Valley Authority' contains no parenthetical role text or descriptors.

lead_agency.notes: Tennessee Valley Authority is a well-known federal agency commonly serving as lead for EIS documents and is confirmed via NUL. No cited title pages were provided, so full corroboration is limited to the NUL source alone, which slightly reduces confidence. No role text or joint-lead concerns are present.

rubric_results.check: project_description text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: The cited pages (introduction and chapter pages) confirm 2-unit nuclear plant in Tishomingo County, MS, on Yellow Creek embayment of Pickwick Lake, natural-draft cooling towers, transmission lines, and commercial operation dates of March 1985 and March 1986 per the Introduction text visible.

rubric_results.check: Numbers/places/dates match cited pages
rubric_results.note: March 1985 and March 1986 commercial operation dates confirmed in Introduction. Tishomingo County, Mississippi confirmed on summary sheet. The 1,160-acre site, 1,285-1,339 MW output, 120 miles of transmission, and 1977 construction start are plausible from chapter references but not directly visible in the pulled text; however no contradicting figures appear.

rubric_results.check: affected_community figures (6,125 people, 3.37M visits 1974, 2,600 workers, 470 students) supported
rubric_results.note: These specific figures are cited to pages 49-99, 97-147, 145-195, 241-291 which are referenced in the table of contents (population, socioeconomic sections) but the full text of those pages is not pulled in. Cannot confirm or deny from visible text alone.

rubric_results.check: alternatives_overview ($126M/year cost advantage, Yellow Creek vs Saltillo comparison) supported
rubric_results.note: The summary sheet confirms nuclear chosen over coal for environmental and cost reasons, and Chapter 9 covers site alternatives including candidate sites. The $126M figure and Saltillo faulting/flooding details are cited to pages not fully visible in the pull.

rubric_results.check: environmental_impact figures (440 acres cleared, 3,985 acres disturbed, 105 acres prime farmland, 7-10% larval fish) supported
rubric_results.note: Summary sheet confirms construction turbidity, land conversion, fish larvae impacts, and radiation within limits—consistent with extraction. Specific acreage and percentage figures cited to chapter pages not fully visible.

rubric_results.check: public_response based_on_main_doc_only=true and limited to main doc
rubric_results.note: The extraction references Appendix B and C (responses to comments) and Chapter 12 (approvals/consultations) which are part of the main EIS document. Draft date May 25, 1977 and final date January 30, 1978 confirmed on summary sheet.

rubric_results.check: No invention or hallucinated detail beyond cited content
rubric_results.note: All agencies listed (EPA, CEQ, Interior, Agriculture, HUD, ERDA, Advisory Council on Historic Preservation) appear on the summary sheet's review list. Draft/final dates confirmed. No obviously fabricated details detected; specific numeric claims are plausible and tied to appropriate chapter sections.

summary.notes: The summary sheet and Introduction visible in the pull confirm the core facts: 2-unit nuclear plant in Tishomingo County MS, March 1985/1986 commercial operation dates, nuclear chosen over coal on cost/environmental grounds, agency review list, and draft/final statement dates. Many specific figures (acreages, population counts, cost savings, fish entrainment percentages) are cited to chapter pages whose full text was not pulled, so they cannot be independently verified from the visible content but are not contradicted by anything visible. The extraction is internally consistent and well-anchored to the document structure.

Because no source text was surfaced, none of the substantive rubric checks can be evaluated. A human reviewer should locate the Alternatives chapter in the EIS and either re-run extraction with corrected logic or manually populate the alternatives field.

rubric_results.note: Cannot confirm taxonomy compliance without access to the fixed taxonomy document, but names appear plausible as standard EIS taxonomy entries.

themes.notes: The extraction provides no cited page text, making it impossible to verify that the document summary actually supports the chosen themes and subthemes. The justification narrative is internally coherent and plausible for a nuclear power plant EIS, but without source text the rubric checks cannot be meaningfully completed. A human reviewer should confirm the underlying document content before accepting this extraction.

rubric_results.check: Place names appear in cited pages?
rubric_results.note: The cited pages (1-30, interpreted as the document's early pages including the Summary Sheet and Introduction) clearly reference 'Yellow Creek,' 'Tishomingo County, Mississippi,' and 'Pickwick Lake' multiple times in the Summary Sheet and Introduction sections provided.

rubric_results.check: is_multi_site set correctly?
rubric_results.note: The document describes a single nuclear plant site in Tishomingo County, MS. While transmission line corridors are mentioned, the primary project is a single-site facility. is_multi_site=false is appropriate for the plant location extraction.

rubric_results.check: For corridor projects: endpoints named, not just a midpoint?
rubric_results.note: This is a point-site extraction, not a corridor project. Transmission corridors are ancillary and not the focus of the location field.

rubric_results.check: State abbreviations match U.S. conventions?
rubric_results.note: 'MS' is the correct USPS abbreviation for Mississippi. Used consistently and correctly in the extracted value.

location.notes: The extracted location accurately identifies Yellow Creek, Tishomingo County, MS as the project site, consistent with multiple references in the cited pages. The geocoded coordinates and address align with the known location of the Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant site in northeastern Mississippi on Pickwick Lake. The is_multi_site=false designation is appropriate given the single primary site, even though ancillary transmission corridors exist.

rubric_results.check: Each agency preparer named in the cited Preparers/Consultation text?
rubric_results.note: Harry G. Moore, Jr. is explicitly named on the Summary Sheet as 'Acting Director, Division of Environmental Planning' with contact information for the TVA environmental statement.

rubric_results.check: Cooperating agencies / tribal nations actually listed in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: All 20 cooperating agencies listed in the extraction appear verbatim under item 6 ('Federal agencies to review' and 'State and local agencies to review') on the Summary Sheet page. However, 'Tederal State Programs' in the source text appears to be a typo for 'Federal State Programs' — the extraction correctly renders it as 'Federal State Programs, State of Mississippi'.

rubric_results.check: For public_commenters: stance present on cited page? Quote verbatim? Speaker clear?
rubric_results.note: No public commenters were extracted; the field is empty and no public comment text appears in the cited pages provided.

rubric_results.check: Private individuals: last-name-only or 'private commenter' convention followed?
rubric_results.note: No private individuals were extracted as commenters.

key_people.notes: The single agency preparer (Harry G. Moore, Jr.) and all 20 cooperating/reviewing agencies are accurately extracted from the Summary Sheet of the cited text. No public commenters were identified or extracted, which is consistent with the cited pages provided (no comment-response section appears in the supplied text). The minor OCR typo 'Tederal' in the source is correctly normalized in the extraction.
